docs.title: iolanta
docs.text: MkDocs Iolanta mkdocs-iolanta Static sites generator + Knowledge Graph browser = Mini-framework for Knowledge Graph aware MkDocs plugins

docs.title: Plugins based on mkdocs-iolanta
docs.text: tables Draw tables from YAML & JSON descriptions on MkDocs site pages

docs.text: Class to describe files in a MkDocs installation.

docs.title: mkdocs:title
docs.text:
Alias

This keyword is also available as title, without the mkdocs: prefix.

Define a readable title for:

  • a MkDocs page,
  • a table column,
  • or anything else.
arda-countries.yaml
$id: arda-countries

table:columns:
- $id: country-name
title: Name of the country

table:rows:
- country-name: Angmar
$id: angmar
- country-name: Mordor
- country-name: Gondor
⇒ {{ render('arda-countries') }} Country name Angmar Mordor Gondor

docs.title: schema:url
docs.text:
Assign a URL to something and then render it with a clickable link.

url.md
---
title: "schema:url"
$included:
$id: link-table
table:columns:
- link
table:rows:
- link:
title: boo
schema:url: https://boo.com
---

Assign a URL to something and then render it with a clickable link.

{{ render('link-table', environments='side-by-side') }}
⇒ {{ render('link-table') }} Link file:///home/runner/work/iolanta-tech/iolanta-tech/docs/url.md/b2

docs.title: Bool
docs.text:
Render boolean values.

bool.md
---
title: Bool
$included:
$id: bool-table
table:columns:
- boolean-value
table:rows:
- boolean-value: true
- boolean-value: false
- boolean-value: yes
- boolean-value: no
---

Render boolean values.

{{ render('bool-table', environments='side-by-side') }}

## Code
iolanta.facets.html.bool_literal.BoolLiteral
⇒ {{ render('bool-table') }} Boolean value ✔️ ❌ ✔️ ❌

docs.title: Code
docs.text: iolanta.facets.html.bool_literal.BoolLiteral

docs.title: iolanta-jinja2
docs.text: pip install iolanta-jinja2

docs.title: {{ render(…) }}
docs.text:
Render a link to an MkDocs page, a table, or any other object in iolanta ecosystem by its identifier.

{{ render('country-name') }}

⇒

Country name

The system automatically determines type of the referenced object and the methods to render it with.

docs.title: macros plugin
docs.text: mkdocs-macros-plugin must be configured at mkdocs.yml for render() to work. See Install for details.

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text: Define a few special keywords (beginning from $) which are particularly useful to use the tool.

docs.title: $id
docs.text:
Defines a unique identifier for something. Identifiers are case sensitive. For instance:

Our cats table is now globally identified as my-cats. You can render it on any page of the site by this name:

simple.yaml
$id: my-cats

table:columns:
- name
- color
- birthday

table:rows:
- name: Ray
color: Red
birthday: 2010-01-01
- name: Smoky
color: Gray
birthday: 2010-12-10

table:order-by: birthday
⇒ {{ render('my-cats') }} Name Color Birthday Ray Red 2010-01-01 Smoky Gray 2010-12-10

docs.title: Other uses
docs.text:
Unique identifiers can be assigned to rows, columns, and generally to any node of Iolanta graph. For instance:

arda-countries.yaml
$id: arda-countries

table:columns:
- $id: country-name
title: Name of the country

table:rows:
- country-name: Angmar
$id: angmar
- country-name: Mordor
- country-name: Gondor
⇒ {{ render('arda-countries') }} Country name Angmar Mordor Gondor

Here, country-name becomes a global identifier. Check Render to see how to use it.

docs.title: Prefixes
docs.text:
Identifiers can have prefixes. For instance, table:columns is an identifier which has table: prefix. That prefix is associated with a URL:

  • table: = https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/
  • and therefore table:columns = https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/columns

whence you can find a page describing what table:columns really means ;)

This self-documenting trait of identifiers is widely used in Iolanta ecosystem.

docs.title: $type
docs.text:
Define type for something. This is most useful in conjunction with https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/class:

samples/star-trek-characters.yaml
- name: Spock
origin: 🖖 Vulcan
shirt-color: 🔵
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: James T. Kirk
origin: 🌎 Earth / West
shirt-color: 🟡
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Leonard McCoy
origin: 🌎 Earth / West
shirt-color: 🔵
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Montgomery Scott
origin: 🌍 Earth / East
shirt-color: 🔴
rank: Chief Engineer
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Pavel Chekhov
origin: 🌍 Earth / East
shirt-color: 🟡
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Niyota Uhura
origin: 🌍 Earth / East
shirt-color: 🔴
rank: Communications Officer
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Hikaru Sulu
origin: 🌍 Earth / East
shirt-color: 🟡
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Christine Chapel
origin: 🌍 Earth / East
shirt-color: 🔵
$type: StarTrekCharacter
- name: Janice Rand
origin: 🌎 Earth / West
shirt-color: 🔴
$type: StarTrekCharacter

and

star-trek-characters-ordered.yaml
$id: star-trek-characters-ordered

table:columns:
- name
- origin
- shirt-color

table:class: StarTrekCharacter

table:order-by:
- origin
- shirt-color
⇒ {{ render('star-trek-characters-ordered') }} Name Origin Shirt color Spock 🖖 Vulcan 🔵 James T. Kirk 🌎 Earth / West 🟡 Leonard McCoy 🌎 Earth / West 🔵 Montgomery Scott 🌍 Earth / East 🔴 Pavel Chekhov 🌍 Earth / East 🟡 Niyota Uhura 🌍 Earth / East 🔴 Hikaru Sulu 🌍 Earth / East 🟡 Christine Chapel 🌍 Earth / East 🔵 Janice Rand 🌎 Earth / West 🔴

docs.title: iolanta-tables
docs.text:
While Markdown is a very readable and lightweight document format, one of its weak points are tables. Markdown table formatting is hard, and a table once written is hard to change. Some Markdown users even give up and write their tables in HTML.

This plugin for MkDocs provides facilities to specify table data and formats using simple YAML markup.

docs.title: Demo
docs.text:
I've got two cats.

simple.yaml
$id: my-cats

table:columns:
- name
- color
- birthday

table:rows:
- name: Ray
color: Red
birthday: 2010-01-01
- name: Smoky
color: Gray
birthday: 2010-12-10

table:order-by: birthday
⇒ {{ render('my-cats') }} Name Color Birthday Ray Red 2010-01-01 Smoky Gray 2010-12-10

Let's look deeper into the code underneath that table.

samples/simple.yaml
$id: my-cats

table:columns:
- name
- color
- birthday

table:rows:
- name: Ray
color: Red
birthday: 2010-01-01
- name: Smoky
color: Gray
birthday: 2010-12-10

table:order-by: birthday

  1. Unique ID of your table. By this ID, you can call the table at any place on the site.

  2. List of columns in its simplest form. Order of columns is preserved.

  3. Data to build the table from. Order is not preserved.

  4. Column(s) to order the data by.

Want some more? See Install.

docs.title: table:class
docs.text:
Just as https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/rows, this keyword permits to specify data for the table, but it does so from a different perspective.

jedi.yaml
- $id: jedi
table:class: Jedi
table:columns:
- name

- name: Yoda
$type: Jedi

- name: Anakin Skywalker
$type: Jedi

- name: Obi-wan Kenobi
$type: Jedi
⇒ {{ render('jedi') }} Name Yoda Anakin Skywalker Obi-wan Kenobi

Here, we define several objects of Jedi class using $type keyword. Having done that, we can instruct the table to render all instances of that class.

Note that we don't have to define the class itself anyhow. The system is smart enough to guess that Jedi is a class from the fact that we used it for $type keyword.

docs.title: table:columns
docs.text: List columns of a table. Order is preserved.

docs.title: Shorthand form
docs.text:
samples/simple.yaml
# ✂ ⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯
table:columns:
- name
- color
- birthday
# ✂ ⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯

Every column listed in this format is a global identifier (is interpreted as $id). However, in this form you cannot define title or other properties of a column. For that, the longhand form is required.

docs.title: Lengthy form
docs.text:
samples/arda-countries.yaml
# ✂ ⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯
table:columns:
- $id: country-name
title: Name of the country
# ✂ ⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯

The nature of country-name as a global identifier is now revealed.

docs.title: table:direction
docs.text: Belongs to https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/order-by section and defines ordering direction.

docs.title: Values
docs.text:
• asc (the default),
  • desc

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
star-trek-characters-ordered-desc.yaml
$id: star-trek-characters-ordered-desc

table:columns:
- name
- origin
- shirt-color

table:class: StarTrekCharacter

table:order-by:
- origin:
table:direction: desc
table:empty-values: first
- shirt-color
⇒ {{ render('star-trek-characters-ordered-desc') }} Name Origin Shirt color Spock 🖖 Vulcan 🔵 James T. Kirk 🌎 Earth / West 🟡 Leonard McCoy 🌎 Earth / West 🔵 Montgomery Scott 🌍 Earth / East 🔴 Pavel Chekhov 🌍 Earth / East 🟡 Niyota Uhura 🌍 Earth / East 🔴 Hikaru Sulu 🌍 Earth / East 🟡 Christine Chapel 🌍 Earth / East 🔵 Janice Rand 🌎 Earth / West 🔴

docs.title: table:empty-values
docs.text: Belongs to https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/order-by section and defines ordering for empty (missing) values.

docs.title: Values
docs.text:
• first (the default),
  • last

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
star-trek-characters-ordered-nullable.yaml
$id: star-trek-characters-ordered-nullable

table:columns:
- name
- origin
- rank

table:class: StarTrekCharacter

table:order-by:
- origin
- rank:
table:empty-values: last
⇒ {{ render('star-trek-characters-ordered-nullable') }} Name Origin Rank Spock 🖖 Vulcan James T. Kirk 🌎 Earth / West Leonard McCoy 🌎 Earth / West Montgomery Scott 🌍 Earth / East Chief Engineer Pavel Chekhov 🌍 Earth / East Niyota Uhura 🌍 Earth / East Communications Officer Hikaru Sulu 🌍 Earth / East Christine Chapel 🌍 Earth / East Janice Rand 🌎 Earth / West

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install iolanta

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml
# …
plugins:
- search
- # … whatever you want
- mkdocs-iolanta-tables
- macros:
on_error_fail: true
modules:
- mkdocs_iolanta.macros

docs.title: table:order-by
docs.text:
Specifies the column we are sorting table contents by.

simple.yaml
$id: my-cats

table:columns:
- name
- color
- birthday

table:rows:
- name: Ray
color: Red
birthday: 2010-01-01
- name: Smoky
color: Gray
birthday: 2010-12-10

table:order-by: birthday
⇒ {{ render('my-cats') }} Name Color Birthday Ray Red 2010-01-01 Smoky Gray 2010-12-10

docs.title: Multiple fields
docs.text:
star-trek-characters-ordered.yaml
$id: star-trek-characters-ordered

table:columns:
- name
- origin
- shirt-color

table:class: StarTrekCharacter

table:order-by:
- origin
- shirt-color
⇒ {{ render('star-trek-characters-ordered') }} Name Origin Shirt color Spock 🖖 Vulcan 🔵 James T. Kirk 🌎 Earth / West 🟡 Leonard McCoy 🌎 Earth / West 🔵 Montgomery Scott 🌍 Earth / East 🔴 Pavel Chekhov 🌍 Earth / East 🟡 Niyota Uhura 🌍 Earth / East 🔴 Hikaru Sulu 🌍 Earth / East 🟡 Christine Chapel 🌍 Earth / East 🔵 Janice Rand 🌎 Earth / West 🔴

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• Ordering direction
  • Where to put empty cells when sorting?

docs.title: table:rows
docs.text:
Provide data for the table as list of rows, where every row specifies values per column:

samples/simple.yaml
# ✂ ⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯
table:rows:
- name: Ray
color: Red
birthday: 2010-01-01
- name: Smoky
color: Gray
birthday: 2010-12-10
# ✂ ⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯⋯

If a value for a column is missing, it will be rendered as empty. If an extra column (not listed in table:columns) is provided then it will be ignored in this table. (But it can be rendered in other tables or other widgets.)

several-planets.yaml
$id: several-planets

table:columns:
- $id: name
title: Name
- $id: satellite-count
title: Satellite Count

table:rows:
- name: Earth
satellite-count: 1
- name: Venus
clouds-consist-of: acid
⇒ {{ render('several-planets') }} Name Satellite count Earth 1 Venus

docs.title: table:self
docs.text:
Use this as a column (in https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/columns and perhaps in https://mkdocs.iolanta.tech/tables/order-by) to render a representation of the whole row instead of a particular column.

self.yaml
$id: self

table:class: iolanta:File
table:columns:
- table:self
- schema:url
⇒ {{ render('self') }} https://schema.org/url file:///opt/hostedtoolcache/Python/3.11.2/x64/lib/python3.11/site-packages/iolanta/data/facets.yaml docs.title: Facets
docs.text:
• Table instance
    • Environment for:
      • TableHead facet,
        • Which is environment for Th facet,
      • TableBody facet,
        • Which is environment for Tr facet.
